sticky.shortcuts: Shortcuts: ⌘/Ctrl + C copy · ⌘/Ctrl + V paste · ⌘/Ctrl + X cut · Delete / Backspace remove. Shortcuts act on the sticky object — click into the text box (or double-click the sticky on the canvas) to edit its text.

config.sectionStrategyHint: Per-canvas Y-axis labels and the X-axis competition factors. Strategy / portfolio canvases only.
config.sectionPinsHint: Each class is one company / theme / persona; same-class pins auto-connect with a line.
config.sectionStickiesHint: Per-canvas meaning for each sticky colour. Empty rows are hidden from the canvas.
config.yAxisHint: Per-canvas labels — overrides the template defaults. Leave a field blank to fall back.

identity.title: Welcome

identity.editPrompt: This name appears on every sticky and audit entry you create.

business-model-environment.tagline: Map the four external forces shaping your business model

customer-journey.tagline: Map a customer's experience over time — find the dips, design the lifts

blue-ocean-strategy-canvas.tagline: Plot a value curve per company across competitive factors — Blue Ocean Strategy

design-criteria-canvas.tagline: Turn vision, research, and constraints into decision standards

intro.title: Use strategy methods to read the market logic behind cases
intro.body: Strategy methods are **analysis frameworks**, not single business models. Start here to understand the method, then open linked cases to see how companies reconstruct markets through value curves, factors of competition, noncustomers, and strategic trade-offs. The first method is **Blue Ocean Strategy**.

strategyFramework.descriptionEmpty: No description authored for this strategy method yet.

intro.title: Test risky assumptions before you build
intro.body: Each card is a cheap, falsifiable test of ONE risky assumption. Pick one based on **theme** (Discovery is cheaper / weaker evidence; Validation is costlier / stronger), the **risk** it tests (Desirability / Feasibility / Viability), and the **cost band** your team can afford. Every experiment lists the canvases it most often validates — claims you've already written on Value Proposition Canvas, Empathy Map, Customer Journey, BMC, etc. To run one, open the card and click "Use this experiment" — we'll spin up a fresh Experiment Canvas with the method already filled in.

experiments.useThisExperiment: Use this experiment to start a new canvas
experiments.seedFailed: Couldn't pre-fill the experiment — start the canvas blank and add the method manually.

picker.title: Use this experiment in...
picker.modeNewHint: Spin up a fresh project with this experiment as its first canvas.
picker.modeExistingHint: The experiment canvas will be added to the project you choose.
